storage_key=9709/s20_qp_51/questions/q01.png; page-chain projection and OCR evidence reviewed for topic mapping. Snippet: 1 The score when two fair six-sided dice are thrown is the sum of the two numbers on the upper faces. (a) Show that the probability that the score is 4 is \frac{1}{12}. The two dice are thrown repeatedly until a score of 4 is obtained. The number of throws taken is denoted by the random variable X. (b) Find the mean of X. (c) Find the probability that a score of 4 is first obtained on the 6th throw. (d) Find P(X < 8).
